title: Night Out

jokes.text: Michael got gum in his hair crawling under Stanley's car for what turned out to be tinfoil
jokes.setup: Michael discovers something shiny under Stanley's car
jokes.punchline: Gets gum in his hair for a piece of worthless tinfoil
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael impulsiveness leading to embarrassing consequence. Well-structured physical comedy with good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh wow, what is that? Is that... is that shiny?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Stanley, Stanley, move your car. There's something under here.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: Michael, leave it alone.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no, I got it. I got it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it's tinfoil! Guys, look at this tinfoil! It's like a treasure!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not treasure, Michael. That's just trash.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's shiny! It's worth something!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! What the—there's gum in my hair!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have gum in my hair for a piece of worthless tinfoil!

jokes.text: But, best-case scenario, you thought it was a quarter.
jokes.setup: Michael's elaborate explanation of crawling under a car for something shiny
jokes.punchline: Jim points out the maximum value would have been 25 cents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan observation. Excellent economy - devastatingly simple logic delivered with characteristic understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So the most you could have found was a quarter?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: ...Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And you crawled under a car for that.

jokes.text: Kill me, right now.
jokes.setup: Jim's devastating quarter observation
jokes.punchline: Michael's melodramatic death wish over minor embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael overreaction. Good character work but somewhat predictable response.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I wish I was dead.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I wish I was dead right now.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I wish I was dead.

jokes.text: We have peanut butter in the kitchen. I don't feel like peanut butter. Get me an ice cream sandwich. Nope, not for you, it's for your hair.
jokes.setup: Michael wants ice cream sandwich, seems like typical demanding behavior
jokes.punchline: It's actually a practical solution for removing gum from hair
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke with practical absurdity. Solid structure though not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I need an ice cream sandwich. Now.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael, you can't just demand ice cream whenever you want.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's not for me. I got gum stuck in my hair.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Oh. Why didn't you just say that?

jokes.text: Dwight, not the good peanut butter... People are gonna get mad.
jokes.setup: Dwight helping with Michael's hair crisis
jokes.punchline: Jim's concern about office politics over condiment hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Good office mundane absurdity. Jim's practical concern about workplace dynamics is characteristic.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wow, okay, so we're just gonna ignore the fact that Dwight has been using the good hair gel from the executive bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's not theft, Jim. It's resource optimization.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Right, and I'm sure that's what you'll tell Michael when he realizes his hair looks like a porcupine had a bad day.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But honestly, my real concern is that we're about to have a full office civil war over condiment placement in the break room fridge. That's what keeps me up at night.

jokes.text: This is my hair we're talking about.
jokes.setup: Concern about using the good peanut butter
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic prioritization of his vanity over office harmony
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-importance. Good character integration though predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone, I need to address something. We have a serious problem in this office. Someone has been using the good peanut butter. The Skippy Super Chunk. That's not for everyday use. That's for special occasions.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Do you know what I had to do this morning? I had to use the store brand. Store. Brand. Do you understand what that does to my image? I'm the regional manager. People look at me. I set the tone for this office.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: If I'm eating generic peanut butter, what does that say? That says Michael Scott has given up. That says the standards of Dunder Mifflin have slipped. And I will not have that on my watch.

jokes.text: Smells good. Taste good, too. Oh, don't, that's disgusting. Lot of calories.
jokes.setup: Dwight applying peanut butter to Michael's hair
jokes.punchline: Dwight tastes the peanut butter from Michael's hair
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross Dwight behavior. The calorie comment adds absurd practical concern to the disgusting act.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Mmm, creamy.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's my hair, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's nice. Keep massaging, please.
jokes.setup: Dwight treating Michael's gum-in-hair situation
jokes.punchline: Michael enjoying the attention inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Michael making an innocent situation inappropriately intimate. Classic cringe territory.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Hold still. I'm going to cut this out.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, but be careful. This is my hair we're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I know what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, Dwight, this is actually kind of nice. Someone taking care of me like this.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's just gum, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, but still. It's intimate. We should do this more often.

jokes.text: Like a piece of fine art by... any number of renowned artists. Or an arty photograph... of Cindy Crawford nude.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining men are visual creatures who crave beauty
jokes.punchline: His 'sophisticated' art reference devolves to celebrity nudity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pseudo-intellectual posturing. Great structure - starts highbrow, crashes to lowbrow. The awkward pause before 'nude' is gold.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Men are visual creatures. We crave beauty.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's why I appreciate fine art. You know, Michelangelo, da Vinci...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And of course, the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue.

jokes.text: They just lack a certain... Crawfordness.
jokes.setup: Michael struggling to articulate what's wrong with his dates
jokes.punchline: Invents 'Crawfordness' as a quality
jokes.explanation: Clever callback and word creation. Michael's attempt at sophisticated terminology perfectly captures his character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: There's just something... I don't know. It's like a quality that some women have. It's hard to explain.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's not about looks, it's not about personality. It's... Crawfordness.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, like Joan Crawford. That quality.

jokes.text: If the website had been working.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining why they need to redo work
jokes.punchline: Casually admits his website doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan revelation of incompetence. Ryan's casual admission of failure is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: We're gonna have to redo all of this work.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: My website doesn't work.

jokes.text: Watch your back, Jim. I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Ryan suggesting Jim talked to David Wallace about problems
jokes.punchline: Fake-casual threat followed by immediate backtrack
jokes.explanation: Ryan's insecurity showing through attempted intimidation. The quick backtrack reveals his weakness.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Did you go talk to David Wallace about your problems?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Because if you did, I would kill you. But like, not really. I'd just be very upset.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay...

jokes.text: or makes fun of his height or his half-beard...
jokes.setup: Michael warning staff not to hurt Ryan emotionally
jokes.punchline: Specifically mentions Ryan's physical insecurities while trying to protect him
jokes.explanation: Michael's well-intentioned but counterproductive protection. Drawing attention to insecurities while trying to prevent mockery.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, I need you to be very careful with Ryan. He's going through a lot right now, and I don't want anyone saying anything that might hurt him emotionally. No comments about his appearance, his clothes, his hair—nothing. He's very sensitive about those things.

jokes.text: Yes, the social networking feature of the Dunder-Mifflin Infinity website was infiltrated by sexual predators.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if police solved 'the problem'
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of serious criminal activity on their website
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. The casual delivery of horrifying news about their paper company's social network is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, the police came by and they were like, 'We're going to shut down your website.' And I was like, 'Why?' And they were like, 'Because you're committing credit card fraud.' And I was like, 'Oh.'

jokes.text: And then an older gentleman asks you, 'boxers or briefs?'
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining the website's social features
jokes.punchline: Dwight references the sexual predator problem with specific example
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with specific creepy example. Dwight's literal interpretation of the problem is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: The website has all these social features - you can message people, share photos, connect with friends...

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Sounds like a sexual predator's dream. Which is why I've already created seventeen fake profiles.

jokes.text: If I had created a website with this many problems, I'd kill myself.
jokes.setup: Discussion of website problems
jokes.punchline: Kevin's matter-of-fact suicide threat over professional failure
jokes.explanation: Kevin's blunt honesty cutting through corporate BS. Perfect economy and dark humor delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I've been trying to fix the website all morning. At this point, if one more thing goes wrong, I'm just going to... well, let's just say I won't be coming back from my lunch break.

jokes.text: Number one: how dare you?
jokes.setup: Kelly saying she has a lot of questions about Ryan's presentation
jokes.punchline: Her first 'question' is pure emotional outrage, not actually a question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment. The misdirection from 'questions' to emotional attack is beautifully structured and became highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I have a lot of questions. Number one: How dare you?

jokes.text: And I'm not applauding... sarcastically.
jokes.setup: Kelly defending Ryan's work
jokes.punchline: She clarifies her non-clapping isn't sarcastic, making it obviously sarcastic
jokes.explanation: Kelly's self-aware but still revealing comment. The denial makes the sarcasm more obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I'm not being sarcastic. I genuinely think Ryan's work is... fine.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: And when I don't clap, it's not sarcasm. It's just... I'm tired.

jokes.text: But now, at a time TDB, all of these problems will be in the past.
jokes.setup: Kelly defending Ryan's timeline
jokes.punchline: She says 'TDB' (to be determined) as if it's an actual time
jokes.explanation: Kelly misunderstanding corporate speak as an actual time is perfectly absurd and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: No, Ryan's timeline is perfect. He said he'd call me back at TDB.

jokes.text: That's still going on?
jokes.setup: Jim says he's dating Pam
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive surprise that their relationship continues
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual dismissal of Jim and Pam's relationship shows his self-absorption. Good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh wow. Jim and Pam? Still? I thought that was just a phase.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Like when I dated that girl with the lazy eye. Wait, that was two years.

jokes.text: Old ball and chain's been a lot more chain than ball lately, if you know what I'm saying. I'm right here.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to join the guys' night out
jokes.punchline: Insults his girlfriend while she's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy obliviousness. The cringe of insulting your partner in front of them is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey guys, can I join you for guys' night?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh come on, she won't even notice I'm gone. She's basically a houseplant with a pulse.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you're proving my point.

jokes.text: Probably wearing the same clothes that I'm wearing right now. If you catch my drift.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he'll see everyone tomorrow morning
jokes.punchline: Implying he'll be too busy having sex to change clothes
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate signaling about sexual conquest. Predictable but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'll see everyone tomorrow morning. I'm gonna be wearing the same clothes because I'll be too busy having sex to change.

jokes.text: I am going... to go get laid. Good-bye! With sex!
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound suave about his intentions
jokes.punchline: Completely undermines himself with awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at confidence crashing into awkwardness. The redundant clarification is perfectly Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not just here for the sex. I mean, don't get me wrong, the sex is great. But I'm also here for the... the emotional connection. And the sex.

jokes.text: The one time a year they hear one.
jokes.setup: Jim saying the group respects good ideas
jokes.punchline: Implies good ideas are extremely rare in this office
jokes.explanation: Jim's dry self-deprecation about office competence. Good economy and characteristic delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: The group respects good ideas. Which is why we've never had to worry about hurt feelings.

jokes.text: Swingers. Classic. Jon Favreau, tall guy from Dodgeball.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing attractive people at a club
jokes.punchline: Confused movie reference showing his cultural confusion
jokes.explanation: Michael's pop culture confusion. Mixes up Swingers with Dodgeball, showing his scattered references.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Look at all these people. This is like... Avatar.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Except instead of blue people, it's just regular people. And instead of a planet, it's a club.

jokes.text: I don't go to a place like that to see more poverty, you know? You go there to get some glamour...
jokes.setup: Discussion about Cabo
jokes.punchline: Privileged person complaining about poverty ruining their luxury vacation
jokes.explanation: Satirical take on privileged tourist mentality. Shows the shallow people Michael wants to impress.

dialogue.speaker: Club patron
dialogue.line: I was in Cabo last week, and there were all these poor people everywhere. It totally ruined my vacation.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, guys! Good job! Later.
jokes.setup: Michael and Dwight arrive at Ryan's cool club
jokes.punchline: Ryan immediately dismisses them like employees after pretending to be excited
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan fake-friendliness turning to dismissal. The boss-employee dynamic flipped and made cruel.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Oh hey! Michael, Dwight! So great to see you guys! Welcome to the club!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Ryan! This place is amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, thanks for coming. Anyway, I'm gonna have to get back to my friends now. See you guys later.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: But we just got here.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I know, but like, I'm kind of in the middle of something important. You understand.

jokes.text: If I'm not in my bath with a glass of red wine in 1 hour, you're both dead.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam locked out of the office
jokes.punchline: Angela's specific ritual and death threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela prissy routine combined with genuine menace. The specificity makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You know, I have a very specific morning ritual. I arrive at the office at 6:47 AM, I feed my cats, I do my stretches, and then I come in here and I organize my desk. And if anyone interferes with that ritual, I will kill you.

jokes.text: if I'm dead, you guys have been dead for weeks.
jokes.setup: Jim asking what happens if Dwight dies and they need office access
jokes.punchline: Dwight's apocalyptic logic about office hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic. His survivalist worldview applied to office management. Perfect character integration and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What happens if you die? How do we get into the office?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You won't. I have the only key. When I die, the office dies with me. That's the way it should be. I built this place from the ground up. Blood, sweat, and tears. And when I'm gone, it all goes dark.

jokes.text: No, no, not a wizard. A hobbit.
jokes.setup: Someone calling Troy a 'banking wizard'
jokes.punchline: Michael corrects them based on physical appearance, not occupation
jokes.explanation: Michael's literal interpretation missing the metaphor. Typical social awkwardness in a trendy setting.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: He's not a wizard, he's a warlock. Warlocks are real.

jokes.text: Do you live in a regular-sized house?
jokes.setup: Michael talking to Troy (who he thinks looks like a hobbit)
jokes.punchline: Asking if he lives in a normal house vs hobbit hole
jokes.explanation: Michael doubling down on the hobbit thing. Good callback with escalating inappropriateness.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Do you live in a normal house, or like a hobbit hole?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A normal house.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, because you look like you could live in a hobbit hole.

jokes.text: Hey... Chief. This is Jim Halpert from... where you work. You're the guy who sits behind the desk. You're the... the african-american guy.
jokes.setup: Jim calling security guard whose name they don't know
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward attempts at identification ending in racial description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating awkwardness. Jim's normally smooth character fumbling into inappropriate territory is perfect cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, uh, security guard? The tall one? Works nights?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, not that one. The other tall one.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: He's got the... the walkie-talkie? And the uniform?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, he's... he's African American.

jokes.text: They lay their eggs inside the unripe beetroot, then, come springtime, the babies eat their way out.
jokes.setup: Club guy asking about weevils
jokes.punchline: Dwight's grotesquely detailed agricultural explanation in a trendy nightclub
jokes.explanation: Perfect fish-out-of-water moment. Dwight's farming expertise delivered with inappropriate detail in cool club setting.

dialogue.speaker: Club Guy
dialogue.line: So what's a weevil?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A weevil is a small beetle with a elongated snout. They're particularly destructive to cotton crops. The boll weevil, specifically, can devastate an entire harvest. I've studied agricultural pest management extensively. They overwinter in soil and plant debris, emerging in spring to feed on developing cotton bolls. The damage is significant — they create entry holes that allow secondary infections and fiber degradation. In the early 1900s, the boll weevil caused over $200 million in damages across the American South. Modern integrated pest management uses pheromone traps, cultural practices, and targeted insecticide applications to control populations.

jokes.text: By show of hands, who thinks we're a better couple than Jim and Pam?
jokes.setup: Office workers stuck together
jokes.punchline: Angela forcing competitive relationship validation from coworkers
jokes.explanation: Angela's insecurity and need for validation manifested in awkward public polling. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I need everyone to acknowledge that I'm the most organized person in this office.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's objectively false. My filing system is—

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Dwight, no one asked you. I'm talking to people who matter.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Angela, we're kind of busy right now.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I don't care. Say it. Say I'm the most organized.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You are... very organized, Angela.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: With enthusiasm, Pam. I can hear the lie in your voice.

jokes.text: Back to the Future. 'Back' because it's on my back. And 'future' because I'm the kind of guy who likes to look ahead into the future.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his tattoo idea
jokes.punchline: Painfully literal explanation of wordplay that isn't clever
jokes.explanation: Michael thinking he's clever with obvious wordplay. The over-explanation kills any wit, which is perfect for his character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm thinking of getting a tattoo that says 'Laugh Out Loud' but instead of LOL, it's just written out as the full words.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's redundant, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. See, because people will see it and they'll laugh out loud. Get it?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not how tattoos work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It absolutely is.

jokes.text: Back to the Future?! Well, you should take a film education course.
jokes.setup: Young woman never heard of Back to the Future
jokes.punchline: Michael's condescending response to generational gap
jokes.explanation: Michael's age showing through cultural references. His pompous response to not knowing a movie is characteristic.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You've never seen Back to the Future? What is wrong with you? That came out in 1985. That's like, before you were born. Actually, that makes sense now.

jokes.text: This place is like a... like a sexy preschool.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing how young everyone at the club is
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable comparison involving children and sexuality
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and inappropriate Michael observation. The comparison is both accurate and disturbing.

jokes.text: Amazons!
jokes.setup: Seeing tall female basketball players
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mythological reference to tall women
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight classical reference. His warrior mentality applied to attractive women.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: These women are like Amazons. Except they're real, and they can play basketball.

jokes.text: Nice try! I've never met anybody who does that.
jokes.setup: Someone sending Michael a drink
jokes.punchline: His paranoid reaction and awkward compliment about drug testing drinks
jokes.explanation: Michael's paranoia and social awkwardness. Treating a friendly gesture as suspicious.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Who sent this? Is this from corporate? Did I do something wrong? Should I be worried?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what, this is actually really thoughtful. Very nice presentation. I'm going to take a sip... but first, let me just say, whoever sent this clearly has excellent taste in beverages and an impressive ability to drug test drinks.

jokes.text: Ryan told me to always tell a woman you work in finance.
jokes.setup: Dwight talking to a woman about his job
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's lying about his job on Ryan's advice
jokes.explanation: Dwight's literal following of advice, ruining the deception by explaining it. Classic Dwight social incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm the Assistant Regional Manager at Dunder Mifflin.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: That sounds impressive.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, actually, Ryan told me to say that. I'm just a salesman.

jokes.text: She washes dogs! I know! I don't wanna get ahead of myself, but... I think I want her to meet my mom.
jokes.setup: Dwight excited about meeting a woman
jokes.punchline: Going from 'she washes dogs' to wanting her to meet his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight romantic escalation. From mundane job to life-changing relationship in seconds.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: She washes dogs. But I'm thinking about introducing her to my mother.

jokes.text: Please don't pick up. Hello? Hank... Is that you? Yeah. Still haven't left the house yet, huh? I'm getting ready to leave. Good. Please hurry. Stop calling me so I can put on my damn socks.
jokes.setup: Jim desperately calling security guard
jokes.punchline: Guard's annoyed response about putting on socks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation vs. mundane tasks. The sock detail makes it relatable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, Hank! Security! I need your help!

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Jim, it's 3 in the morning. I'm trying to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I know, I know, but this is an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What is it? Is there a break-in?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Worse. I can't find my car keys.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Jim... you called me at 3 AM because you lost your keys? Why don't you just put on some socks and deal with it in the morning?

jokes.text: One of my friends is getting beaten up by some girls!
jokes.setup: Michael on the phone with his mom
jokes.punchline: Has to hang up because Dwight is being attacked by women
jokes.explanation: Absurd situation of adult man being beaten up by women. Good physical comedy setup.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Mom, I have to go. Dwight is being attacked by women.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, I'm not exaggerating. There are literally women all over him right now.

jokes.text: I... am moving to Costa Rica. Thought about it... for a long time now. And I'm finally gonna do it.
jokes.setup: Toby making an announcement to locked-out coworkers
jokes.punchline: Random major life decision announced in parking lot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment. Awkward timing and delivery of major life change. Classic character work.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Everyone, I have an announcement. I've decided to move back to New York.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, what? Right now?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Yes. I'm leaving Dunder Mifflin, effective immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This is highly irregular.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I've also decided to get married. To someone I met on the internet three weeks ago.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Toby, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: So... I'm just gonna hop the fence and jog home then.
jokes.setup: Toby's dramatic Costa Rica announcement
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates to mundane fence-hopping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation from dramatic to mundane. Toby's character perfectly captured in the transition.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'm moving to Costa Rica. It's something I've always dreamed about.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wow, that's... that's great, Toby.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'll be living off the grid, in a small village, completely self-sufficient.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Actually, I'll be staying in a gated community. Very nice amenities. There's a golf course.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: And I've already mapped out which fences I'll be able to hop over on my jogs.

jokes.text: Why are you assuming they only speak spanish? I just... If they speak spanish.
jokes.setup: Approaching cleaning crew for help
jokes.punchline: Oscar calling out Jim's assumption, Jim's awkward justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Oscar's awareness and Jim's unconscious bias. Realistic awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, can you guys help us clean up this mess?

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Why are you assuming we know how to clean? That's a stereotype.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What? No, I just... you work here. I thought everyone could—I mean, we all need to pitch in...

jokes.text: Okay? It happens they speak spanish. Lucky us.
jokes.setup: Jim's assumption about cleaning crew being called out
jokes.punchline: His defensive response when proven right
jokes.explanation: Jim's slightly defensive vindication. Good character moment showing he's not perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I wasn't saying anything about the cleaning crew.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I was just making an observation.

jokes.text: Why don't you take your clothes off? No!
jokes.setup: Troy's apparent drug problem being discussed
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate suggestion followed by Ryan's panicked refusal
jokes.explanation: Generic uncomfortable situation. Less character-specific than most Office humor.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? Just try some of my stuff. It'll help you relax.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm good. I'm totally good.

jokes.text: And then you put a wire on him... and you find out who's selling him... drugs. And then you get that guy and you flip him. You turn him into a snitch.
jokes.setup: Giving advice about friend's drug problem
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate undercover police operation plan
jokes.explanation: Michael's TV-informed advice taken to absurd extremes. Good character moment showing his unrealistic worldview.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, here's what we're gonna do. We go undercover. I'll be a drug dealer named... Vincenzo. You'll be my muscle, Tiny. We'll set up a fake operation, gain their trust, and then BAM! We bust 'em. It's foolproof. I've seen every episode of Miami Vice.

jokes.text: I've been watching The Wire recently... I don't understand a word of it.
jokes.setup: Michael giving elaborate police advice
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't understand his source material
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment. Gives confident advice based on something he doesn't understand. Great callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: See, what you do is you find the guy, you look him in the eye, and you say 'I know what you did.' Works every time.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's from a movie, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I don't know which one.

jokes.text: Like a famous person once said, 'boys on the side.' But I don't... I disagree. I say, let's hear it for the boys.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on friendship
jokes.punchline: Confuses movie titles and song lyrics while making pseudo-profound point
jokes.explanation: Michael's cultural confusion creating pseudo-wisdom. Mixes up references while trying to sound deep.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, friendship is like... it's like that movie, 'Sleepless in Seattle.' Except it's more like 'Sleepless in the Office.' And the song goes, 'Don't stop believin'... hold on to that feelin'... friendship.' See? That's deep. That's what friendship is about. Not giving up. Like in 'Rocky.' Or was it 'Rocky Balboa'? Same thing. The point is, you gotta have friends who believe in you, and you believe in them, and together you're like... you're like a bad action movie sequel that somehow makes money. That's friendship.

tmdb_overview: Ryan returns to Scranton but not to a warm welcome. He is making everyone in the branch come in on a Saturday to re-enter data into the new website. Michael makes it clear that Ryan is the reason they have to come in on the weekend. The rest of the staff aren't happy knowing a computer will be getting credit for all of their sales.
insight: Cringe escalation drives Season 4's tightest drunk-night episode at 2.42 jokes per minute.
